chapter_goal: Explain Marsden-Weinstein-Meyer reduction as regular level plus null-foliation quotient plus induced closed symplectic form.
computational_checks: rank dmu = dim G at the chosen C^2 zero-level point
orbit vector lies in ker dmu and is isotropic
row span of dmu agrees with contraction of the orbit vector into omega up to convention
quotient basis on I^omega/I has nondegenerate skew matrix
all generated artifacts exist and have nonzero size
concept_inventory: MWM hypotheses: hamiltonian compact G-space, zero level, free action
regular value consequence from im dmu_p = annihilator of stabilizer
orbit tangent as isotropic subspace inside the zero level
linear lemma: an isotropic subspace I gives a symplectic quotient I^omega / I
slice theorem gives local quotient charts and principal bundle projection
reduced form is defined by i^* omega = pi^* omega_red and is closed
commuting hamiltonian actions descend when the second moment map is G-invariant

library_routing_table.why: the proof is a dependency chain
learners can inspect where D stays nonzero
the quotient is an exact linear-algebra calculation
S^3 to CP^1 is the smallest faithful geometric model

source_span_read.verification: The extracted text begins with Part IX / Symplectic Reduction and Lecture 23 / The Marsden-Weinstein-Meyer Theorem, then reaches the commuting-action remark on printed page 144.

visual_sequence.validation: graph has a path from hypotheses to reduced closed symplectic form

visual_sequence.inspection_target: how null orbit directions disappear from I^omega/I
visual_sequence.validation: reduced 2-form matrix has rank 2 and determinant 1

visual_sequence.inspection_target: level set, orbit fibers, and CP^1 base
visual_sequence.validation: dimension drop 4-1-1=2 and ker dmu=(T orbit)^omega
